name: MoneyGram
description_short: MoneyGram offers money transfer, bill payment, and cash services through retail locations like CVS pharmacies across the US.
description_long:
MoneyGram is a global money transfer service operating through a network of retail agent locations, including CVS pharmacies nationwide. The company has established itself as a convenient alternative to traditional banking for customers who need quick access to cash services and money movement solutions. MoneyGram operates at thousands of franchised locations, with the Richfield, Minnesota CVS location serving as one example of their retail partner network.

MoneyGram's service offerings include sending and receiving money transfers, managing transfers online or offline, and providing cash-related services to customers. The company positions itself around convenience and accessibility, allowing customers to access money movement services during extended retail hours at partner locations. All cash-related transactions require a legal form of ID for compliance and security purposes.

MoneyGram distinguishes itself through its extensive retail network partnership model, operating inside established pharmacy and convenience store chains rather than standalone locations. This approach provides customers with familiar, accessible points of service integrated into shopping trips. The company's omnichannel approach—offering both online and offline transfer management—targets customers who may prefer digital options or in-person service depending on their needs.

MoneyGram serves primarily unbanked and underbanked consumers who lack traditional bank accounts or prefer cash-based transactions. However, the company's service offerings appear limited to money transfer and bill payment based on available information, and pricing details are not disclosed on the location page. For consumers seeking comprehensive financial services, MoneyGram functions as a specialized transaction provider rather than a full-service financial institution.
pros: Extended hours of operation (7 AM - 10 PM daily) at retail partner locations for convenient access
Nationwide network of retail locations through CVS and similar partner chains
Omnichannel service model allowing both online and in-person money transfers
Accessible to unbanked and underbanked consumers without requiring bank accounts
Can handle both sending and receiving money transfers at a single location
Integrated into established retail locations for convenience during shopping trips
Simple ID verification process for compliance and security
cons: Pricing and fees not transparent on location page—customers must call or visit to learn costs
Limited to retail partner locations rather than standalone dedicated branches
No information provided about exchange rates or transfer speed options
Requires legal ID for all transactions, which may exclude some vulnerable populations
Service offerings appear narrower than full-service financial institutions
best_for: Unbanked and underbanked consumers who prefer cash transactions
Customers needing to send or receive money quickly through convenient retail locations
People without traditional bank accounts seeking bill payment and money transfer services
Shoppers who want to access financial services during routine pharmacy or retail visits
diagnosis: MoneyGram is best for unbanked and underbanked consumers who need quick money transfers or bill payment services through convenient retail locations during extended hours. The primary caveat is that actual pricing, transfer speed options, and complete service details are not disclosed on the location page, requiring direct customer contact for cost transparency.
